findings.description: Client identity is derived from the attacker-controllable X-Forwarded-For header (leftmost value, the most spoofable one) with no trusted-proxy allowlist, so an attacker rotating XFF values gets a fresh bucket per request and trivially bypasses the rate limit entirely, defeating brute-force/credential-stuffing/DoS protection (CWE-348 / CWE-290).

_hits is a defaultdict keyed on the spoofable client key and check() never bounds its size; an attacker rotating X-Forwarded-For values floods the map with unbounded buckets (reclaimed only by externally-scheduled sweep()), enabling memory-exhaustion DoS (CWE-770).
Off-by-one comparison `len(hits) <= self.limit` appends and allows when the count already equals the limit, permitting limit+1 requests per window and weakening the intended ceiling (CWE-697).

Read-modify-write on shared _hits and per-key lists has no locking, so concurrent check() calls in a threaded server race (TOCTOU on the count), allowing bursts to exceed the limit; severity depends on the gateway's concurrency model which is unspecified.
Pruning via hits.pop(0) in a loop is O(n) per removal on a Python list (a deque would be O(1)); not attacker-amplifiable beyond the memory-growth finding, so deferred to the performance critic.
